name: Gauge-mediated SUSY breaking (GMSB)
summary: Mediates SUSY breaking from a hidden sector to the visible sector via SM gauge interactions. Messenger fields charged under both the SUSY-breaking sector and the SM transmit soft masses to MSSM scalars and gauginos at one and two loops. Predicts a flavor-blind soft spectrum (no SUSY flavor problem) and a very light gravitino LSP.

addresses_puzzles.note: Implementation of MSSM SUSY-breaking mediation; addresses the hierarchy problem only via the underlying SUSY mechanism.

Gauge-mediated soft masses are flavor-blind by construction (they depend only on SM gauge quantum numbers), eliminating the SUSY flavor problem. Doesn't explain Yukawa hierarchy itself but removes one source of flavor violation.

GMSB with stau-NLSP: stau decays during BBN deplete Li-7 via stau-bound-state catalysis of nuclear reactions (Jedamzik 2008, Cyburt-Ellis-Fields-Olive 2010). Largely disfavored by collider stau-mass bounds but not strictly excluded.

touches_benchmarks.note: Flavor-blind mediation suppresses CP-violating phases relative to generic MSSM.
FCNCs from squark mixing are minimal due to flavor-universal soft masses.

new_fields: SUSY-breaking sector singlet X with <X> + theta^2 <F_X>
introduces.note: Messenger mass, SUSY-breaking scale, number of messenger generations, tan(beta). Flavor structure is fixed by gauge invariance.

history: Dine and Fischler (1982), 'A phenomenological model of particle physics based on supersymmetry', Phys. Lett. B 110, 227 - one of the foundational gauge-mediation papers. Modern formulation by Dine-Nelson (1993, 1995) and Dine-Nelson-Nir-Shirman (1996). Active LHC phenomenology of long-lived NLSP / prompt photon signatures.

references.citation: Dine and Fischler, 'A phenomenological model of particle physics based on supersymmetry', Phys. Lett. B 110, 227 (1982)
Dine, Nelson, Nir, Shirman, 'New tools for low-energy dynamical supersymmetry breaking', Phys. Rev. D 53, 2658 (1996)
